title: Leymus regelii
taxonomy: Leymus regelii is classified as a species of grass in the genus Leymus. Leymus regelii is a member of the family Poaceae and is closely related to the genus Pseudoroegneria. Within the family Poaceae, Leymus regelii is further classified as a member of the subfamily Pooideae. This species is also part of the tribe Triticeae, which includes other grasses such as wheat and rye. Leymus regelii is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia.

intro: Leymus regelii, also known as Regel's wild rye, is a type of grass native to the mountainous regions of Asia and Europe. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as the ability to help regulate blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Leymus regelii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Leymus regelii is commonly used in traditional medicine and is often prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being incorporated into herbal capsules and supplements.
